title: carum roxburghianum
intro: Carum roxburghianum, also known as Roxburgh's chervil or ajwain, is a herbaceous plant that belongs to the carrot family and is native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its carminative and antispasmodic effects, which help to relieve digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Carum roxburghianum has been associated with several health benefits, including its ability to alleviate symptoms of respiratory disorders such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to support lactation in breastfeeding women. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and powder forms, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
